summary: Unstoppable Garcia on Truckin'→Dark Star→Comes A Time, paint‑peeling close
blurb: Features a blistering Truckin' → Dark Star → Comes A Time sequence and a jazzy Dark Star elsewhere. The second set builds momentum into a spectacular closing Not Fade Away > Great… > Not Fade Away run. Keith's piano adds bright texture while Phil’s backing vocals shine.
review: The July 18, 1972 Roosevelt Stadium show is one of those three‑set marathons that still makes Deadheads’ hearts race. From the opening stretch through a soaring first set, the band hits a high‑water mark with a blistering Truckin' → Dark Star → Comes A Time jam that reviewers call “unstoppable” and “transcendent,” showcasing Jerry’s guitar at perhaps his finest of the era. Keith’s piano weaves a lighter boogie‑woogie feel into the mix, especially noticeable on tracks like Jack Straw and Stella Blue, while Phil’s vocal enthusiasm adds an extra layer of joy to Brown Eyed Women. The second set keeps the momentum rolling, culminating in a paint‑peeling Not Fade Away → Great… → Not Fade Away sequence that left the stadium buzzing. Even with a few static glitches on some soundboard sources, the performance shines through, and the matrix capture (gd1972-07-18.127703.mtx.dusborne.flac16) delivers crystal‑clear clarity that lets every nuance be heard. In short, this show combines monster jams, tight vocal harmonies, and a closing energy that still feels fresh decades later.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of six recordings are marked positive, praising the Truckin'→Dark Star→Comes A Time jam, Jerry's unstoppable guitar, and overall show energy; only one audience source is mixed and no recording notes consistent negative performance criticism appear.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with pristine balance and full show coverage; minimal glitches, captures the dynamic range of the performance.

key_highlights: Blazing Truckin' → Dark Star → Comes A Time jam described as unstoppable.
Jazzy Dark Star segment highlighted for beautiful guitar work.
Paint‑peeling Not Fade Away > Great… > Not Fade Away closing sequence.
Keith's piano adds bright boogie‑woogie texture and lifts the overall sound.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is repeatedly called unstoppable, especially on the Dark Star and Truckin' jams; his tone is described as transcendent and jazzy.
band_performance.Phil: Phil delivers enthusiastic backing vocals (notably on Brown Eyed Women) and a solid bass foundation throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Bob’s vocal contributions are praised as sublime, complementing Jerry’s lead work.
band_performance.Keith: Keith’s piano provides a lighter boogie‑woogie touch, adding new texture to the band’s sound and shining on tracks like Jack Straw and Stella Blue.
band_performance.Billy: Billy’s drumming is likened to Tony Williams in the audience mix, offering a powerful rhythmic backbone.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
